query: AWS DynamoDB on-demand pricing per million write request units storage GB 2025
answer: AWS DynamoDB on-demand pricing for write requests is $1.25 per million standard writes and $2.50 per million transactional writes in 2025. Storage costs are $0.25 per GB-month.

results.title: Amazon DynamoDB Pricing: A 2025 Cost Savings Guide - CloudZero
results.snippet: | Write Request | API calls to write data to your tables   Types: Standard and  Transactional | Write Request Units (WRUs) for On-Demand Mode   Write Capacity Units (WCUs) for Provisioned Mode |. DynamoDB On-Demand capacity mode prices depend on the type of request (read, write, or storage), operation (transactional, eventually consistent, strongly consistent), table class you use (Standard, Standard Infrequent Access), and AWS Region you are based in. **Pricing for data storage in DynamoDB’s On-Demand capacity mode starts at $0.25 per GB-month** for the Standard table class and $0.10 for the Standard Infrequent Access tale class**.**. | DynamoDB Streams | GetRecords API call (streams read request unit) returns up to 1MB of data from Streams | $0.02 per 100,000 streams read request units after the first 2.5 million (which are free every month) |. | DynamoDB Streams | GetRecords API call (streams read request unit) returns up to 1MB of data from Streams | $0.02 per 100,000 streams read request units after the first 2.5 million (which are free every month) |.

results.title: Amazon DynamoDB Pricing: Complete Guide + Calculator | Cloud Burn
results.snippet: Amazon DynamoDB pricing looks simple at first glance: pick a capacity mode, pay for reads, writes, and storage. Your total cost depends on four dimensions: throughput (reads and writes), storage, backups, and add-on features like Global Tables or DAX. The second layer is **add-on features** like storage, backups, DynamoDB Streams, Global Tables, and DAX, which each add incremental costs on top of throughput. A 500 GB table with minimal read/write traffic costs $125/month on Standard vs $50/month on Standard-IA. A Global Table with 3 replicas generating 42.5 million writes per month now costs roughly $8.71/month, down from $26.56 before the price cut. The capacity mode choice has the largest cost impact, and after the November 2024 price cut, on-demand is the right starting point for most workloads. Run your workload through the DynamoDB pricing calculator to compare costs across on-demand, provisioned, and commitment-discounted options before you commit. Database Savings Plans offer up to 18% discount on DynamoDB on-demand throughput and up to 12% on provisioned capacity.

results.title: DynamoDB On-Demand Pricing: Read, Write, and Storage Costs | Uriel Bitton posted on the topic | LinkedIn
results.snippet: # DynamoDB On-Demand Pricing: Read, Write, and Storage Costs. Your monthly bill is based on 3 elements: 1️⃣ 𝗥𝗲𝗮𝗱 𝗥𝗲𝗾𝘂𝗲𝘀𝘁 𝗨𝗻𝗶𝘁𝘀 (𝗥𝗥𝗨𝘀) $0.25 per million RRU (eventually consistent reads) $0.50 per million RRU (strongly consistent reads) $0.50 per million RRU (transactional reads) How RRUs are calculated: A 4 KB item = 1 RRU A read of a 10 KB item = 3 RRUs (because 10 KB → 3 x 4 KB chunks) 💡 Example: If you read 5 million items per month (each <4 KB), cost = 5 × $0.25 = $1.25 2️⃣ 𝗪𝗿𝗶𝘁𝗲 𝗥𝗲𝗾𝘂𝗲𝘀𝘁 𝗨𝗻𝗶𝘁𝘀 (𝗪𝗥𝗨𝘀) $1.25 per million WRU (standard writes) $2.50 per million WRU (transactional writes) How WRUs are calculated: A 1 KB write = 1 WRU A 3 KB write = 3 WRUs Example: If you write 2 million 1-KB items per month: 2 × $1.25 = $2.50 3️⃣ 𝗦𝘁𝗼𝗿𝗮𝗴𝗲 𝗖𝗼𝘀𝘁𝘀 $0.25 per GB-month for table data Indexes (GSIs) also charged at $0.25 per GB-month btw you're billed for compressed storage (DynamoDB internally compresses data) Example: If your table stores 10 GB of data: 10 × $0.25 = $2.50 per month ❓Were you ever confused about DynamoDB pricing? * ### DynamoDB On-Demand Pricing: Exactly How Costs Work. ## Sign in to view more content.

results.title: Understanding DynamoDB Pricing (2025)
results.snippet: DynamoDB charges for **reading, writing, and storing data** in your tables, along with any optional features you choose to enable. It has two capacity modes with their respective billing options for processing reads and writes on your tables: **On-Demand Capacity Mode (auto-scaling)** and **Provisioned Capacity Mode (fixed capacity)**. * **Standard-IA:** Lower storage cost for infrequently accessed data but higher per-operation cost. * **Provisioned Mode:** You specify WCUs (1 KB writes/sec) and RCUs (4 KB strongly consistent reads/sec, or 2 eventually consistent). * **On-Demand Mode:** You pay per request—WRUs for writes and RRUs for reads. On-Demand is better for unpredictable workloads; Provisioned is cheaper for steady traffic. ### On-Demand Mode#. | **Pricing Component** | **On-Demand Mode** | **Provisioned Mode** |. ### Scenario 1: On-Demand Mode#. * **Provisioned**: Lower cost for steady traffic; use **Auto Scaling**. * Use **eventually consistent reads** (half the cost). To keep costs low, choose the right capacity mode, optimize usage, and monitor with AWS tools like CloudWatch and Cost Explorer.

results.title: DynamoDB On-Demand Pricing: Exactly How Costs Work
results.snippet: $1.25 per million WRUs (standard writes); $2.50 per million WRUs (transactional writes). How WRUs are calculated. Writes are charged in 1 KB
